zh.lifeSummary: 當Florence Alice Anslow於1515年出生在英國戴文希德姆斯時，她的父親Hubert Arthur Anslow30歲，母親Myrtle Grace Holland16歲。她於1529年與William Canon在英國戴文希德波利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1567年1月1日在她的家鄉享年52歲，埋葬在英國戴文希德姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Anslow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Anslow in Staffordshire. The placename derives from the Old English female personal name Ēanswīth (genitive Ēanswīthe) + lēah ‘open woodland’. Compare Enslow .

variants.name: Enslow
meanings: English: variant of   Anslow  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Florence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Latin masculine name Florentius (a derivative of florens ‘blossoming, flourishing’) and its feminine form Florentia. In the Middle Ages the name was commonly borne by men (as, for example, the historian Florence of Worcester), but it is now exclusively a girl's name. This was revived in the second half of the 19th century, being given in honour of Florence Nightingale ( 1820–1910 ), the founder of modern nursing, who organized a group of nurses to serve in the Crimean War. She herself received the name because she was born in the Italian city of Florence (Latin Florentia, Italian Firenze).

variants.name: Florance
meanings: Medieval form of the Latin masculine name Florentius (a derivative of florens ‘blossoming, flourishing’) and its feminine form Florentia. In the Middle Ages the name was commonly borne by men (as, for example, the historian Florence of Worcester), but it is now exclusively a girl's name. This was revived in the second half of the 19th century, being given in honour of Florence Nightingale ( 1820–1910 ), the founder of modern nursing, who organized a group of nurses to serve in the Crimean War. She herself received the name because she was born in the Italian city of Florence (Latin Florentia, Italian Firenze).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Florence Alice Anslow was born in 1515, in Sidmouth, Devon, England, her father, Hubert Arthur Anslow, was 30 and her mother, Myrtle Grace Holland, was 16. She married William Canon in 1529, in Sidbury, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died on 1 January 1567, in her hometown, at the age of 52, and was buried in Sidmouth, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Florence Alice Anslow est née en 1515, à Sidmouth, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Hubert Arthur Anslow, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Myrtle Grace Holland, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé William Canon en 1529, à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1567, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Sidmouth, Devon, Angleterre.

ko.Ottery Saint Mary, East Devon district,: 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 세인트 메리 오터이
ko.lifeSummary: Florence Alice Anslow(이)가 1515년 영국 데폰 시드모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hubert Arthur Anslow(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Myrtle Grace Holland(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1529년 영국 데폰 시드버이에서 William Canon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1567년 1월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 시드모우스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Florence Alice Anslow родилась в 1515 г. в местности сидмоут, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Hubert Arthur Anslow было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Myrtle Grace Holland, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Canon в 1529 г. в местности сидбури, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла 1 января 1567 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности сидмоут, девон, Англия.
